setting.note: The novel is anchored in Manhattan, where a bombing at the Metropolitan Museum kills the narrator's mother and leaves him holding a small stolen Dutch painting. From the museum on Fifth Avenue the map runs down to the Park Avenue apartment that takes him in and to the antiques workshop in Greenwich Village that becomes his real home, before the story travels far out to Las Vegas and then to Amsterdam.

rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.

palette.note: The warm gold of the little Dutch bird at the heart of the book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
